title: Amber dark variant coverage, focus:ring cleanup in FollowUpBanner, and font-bold → font-semibold in display stat spans
description: Continuation of the systematic UI improvement loop. This batch adds missing dark: variants to amber indicator dots in KanbanCard, ContactCard, Activity feed, and SettingsPipelineSection Flame icon; adds dark: variant to OfferComparison Trophy icon; removes redundant incomplete focus:ring-ring from FollowUpBanner Button classNames (shadcn Button already handles focus-visible ring); and replaces font-bold with font-semibold on numeric display stat spans in ResumeFitSection and PublicPipeline.

userStories.title: Remove redundant focus:ring-ring from FollowUpBanner Button classNames
userStories.description: As a developer, FollowUpBanner's two Button elements have focus:ring-ring in their className. This is (1) incomplete — no ring-2 width set, (2) uses focus: not focus-visible:, and (3) redundant — the shadcn Button variant already applies focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring internally. Remove it.
acceptanceCriteria: Read src/components/FollowUpBanner.jsx first
On the 'View' Button (line ~29), remove focus:ring-ring from the className string
On the dismiss 'X' Button (line ~33), remove focus:ring-ring from the className string
Do not add any replacement focus classes — the Button component's built-in focus-visible ring is sufficient
If focus:ring-ring is not present (already cleaned up), mark passes: true with note 'already clean'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/components/FollowUpBanner.jsx. Both Button elements at lines 29 and 33 have focus:ring-ring appended to their className. The shadcn Button component (src/components/ui/button.jsx) already includes focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 in its base variants. The partial override is incomplete and misleading — just remove it.

userStories.title: Add dark variant to KanbanCard stale pulse dot (bg-amber-400)
userStories.description: As a developer, KanbanCard's stale indicator dot (line 50) uses bg-amber-400 with no dark variant. The follow-up indicator at line 57 already has dark:bg-amber-900/60. Add the matching dark variant to the stale dot for consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: Read src/components/KanbanCard.jsx first
On the stale span (line ~50) with bg-amber-400 and animate-pulse-dot, add dark:bg-amber-900/40 to match ApplicationRow's stale dot pattern
The follow-up indicator at line ~57 already has dark:bg-amber-900/60 — leave it unchanged
If dark:bg-amber-900/40 (or any dark: variant) is already present, mark passes: true with note 'already has dark variant'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/components/KanbanCard.jsx. The stale span at line 50 has className='absolute right-2 top-2 h-2 w-2 animate-pulse-dot rounded-full bg-amber-400' — no dark variant. ApplicationRow uses dark:bg-amber-900/40 on its stale dot (line 116) — use the same value here. The follow-up indicator at line 57 already has dark:bg-amber-900/60 (intentionally different shade — don't change it).

userStories.title: Add dark variant to ContactCard stale contact dot (bg-amber-400)
userStories.description: As a developer, ContactCard's stale indicator span (line 42) uses bg-amber-400 with no dark: variant, causing it to be too bright in dark mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Read src/components/ContactCard.jsx first
On the stale span (line ~42) with bg-amber-400, add dark:bg-amber-900/40
If dark: variant is already present, mark passes: true with note 'already has dark variant'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/components/ContactCard.jsx. The stale span at line 42 has className='inline-block h-2 w-2 shrink-0 rounded-full bg-amber-400'. Add dark:bg-amber-900/40. The span is wrapped in a Tooltip (already in place) — only the className needs updating.

userStories.title: Add dark variant to Activity.jsx TYPE_STYLES event_created dot color
userStories.description: As a developer, Activity.jsx defines TYPE_STYLES with event_created: 'bg-amber-400' — no dark variant. This dot appears in the activity feed timeline. Add the dark variant to the class string.
acceptanceCriteria: Read src/pages/Activity.jsx first
In the TYPE_STYLES object (line ~22), change event_created value from 'bg-amber-400' to 'bg-amber-400 dark:bg-amber-900/40'
Leave applied: 'bg-primary' and stage_change: 'bg-accent-blue' unchanged (semantic tokens, no change needed)
If dark: variant is already present, mark passes: true with note 'already has dark variant'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/pages/Activity.jsx. TYPE_STYLES at line 22 maps activity type to a dot CSS class string. The dotClass is applied to a small indicator span in the timeline. Only event_created needs the dark variant added — applied uses bg-primary (semantic) and stage_change uses bg-accent-blue (custom token). Use 'bg-amber-400 dark:bg-amber-900/40' as the new value.

userStories.title: Add dark variant to SettingsPipelineSection Flame icon (text-amber-500)
userStories.description: As a developer, the streak section in SettingsPipelineSection uses a Flame icon with text-amber-500 and no dark: variant. ApplicationRow's Bell icon already uses text-amber-500 dark:text-amber-400 — apply the same pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Read src/components/SettingsPipelineSection.jsx first
On the Flame icon (line ~85), change text-amber-500 to text-amber-500 dark:text-amber-400
If dark: variant is already present, mark passes: true with note 'already has dark variant'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/components/SettingsPipelineSection.jsx. The Flame icon at line 85 has className='h-5 w-5 text-amber-500'. Add dark:text-amber-400 to match the project's established pattern for amber icon text (same as ApplicationRow Bell icon at line 138). Single targeted change — only the Flame className.

userStories.title: Add dark variant to OfferComparison Trophy icon (text-amber-500)
userStories.description: As a developer, OfferComparison renders a Trophy icon with text-amber-500 and no dark: variant on the winner column indicator.
acceptanceCriteria: Read src/pages/OfferComparison.jsx first
On the Trophy icon (line ~70), change text-amber-500 to text-amber-500 dark:text-amber-400
If dark: variant is already present, mark passes: true with note 'already has dark variant'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/pages/OfferComparison.jsx. The Trophy icon at line 70 has className='h-4 w-4 text-amber-500'. Add dark:text-amber-400. This is the same amber icon pattern used across the codebase — single targeted addition.

userStories.title: Replace font-bold with font-semibold on ResumeFitSection score span
userStories.description: As a developer, ResumeFitSection displays the fit score with font-bold (line 36). This is a numeric value span, not a heading — font-semibold is the project standard for inline emphasis values.
acceptanceCriteria: Read src/components/ResumeFitSection.jsx first
On the score span at line ~36 (<span className='text-3xl font-bold text-foreground'>), change font-bold to font-semibold
Do NOT change any font-bold on h1/h2/h3 elements or elements with font-display class — those are intentional display typography
If font-semibold is already used, mark passes: true with note 'already using font-semibold'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/components/ResumeFitSection.jsx. The score span at line 36 wraps a numeric score like '87'. It's inline emphasis data — font-semibold matches the project convention for value spans (same fix applied in PRD-53 US-565 for NotificationBell and OfferNegotiationPanel).

userStories.title: Replace font-bold with font-semibold on PublicPipeline StatCard value span
userStories.description: As a developer, PublicPipeline's StatCard component uses font-bold on the stat value span (line 27). Like ResumeFitSection, these are numeric stat displays — font-semibold is standard.
acceptanceCriteria: Read src/pages/PublicPipeline.jsx first
In the StatCard function (line ~27), on the span with text-2xl font-bold text-foreground, change font-bold to font-semibold
Do NOT change font-bold on any heading elements or elements with font-display — those are intentional display typography
If font-semibold is already used, mark passes: true with note 'already using font-semibold'
npm run build passes with no errors
userStories.notes: Read src/pages/PublicPipeline.jsx. StatCard at line 24-31 renders a stat label/value pair. The value span at line 27 uses text-2xl font-bold. Change font-bold to font-semibold. Leave the Pricing.jsx badge (font-bold uppercase tracking-wide) out of scope — that is intentional marketing typography.
